Name: geometric_mixed_precision
Title: Geometric Mixed Precision Optimization with Deterministic Spectral Estimators
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic spectral estimators can be used to develop a training-free mixed precision optimization framework that achieves better accuracy-complexity trade-offs for deep learning models.
Related Work: Recent works such as SNIP, AMP-ViT, and EMQ have explored adaptive mixed precision training and post-training quantization methods. However, these methods rely on heuristic-based approaches or require significant computational overhead. In contrast, our proposed framework leverages geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic spectral estimators to provide a training-free and efficient solution.
Abstract: This paper proposes a novel training-free mixed precision optimization framework for deep learning models. Our approach combines geometric subspace diagnostics with deterministic spectral estimators to efficiently optimize layer-wise precision. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework on various benchmark models, achieving significant reductions in computational complexity while maintaining accuracy. Our framework provides a theoretically grounded and efficient solution for mixed precision optimization, making it an attractive choice for real-world deployments.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed framework on benchmark models such as ResNet and MobileNet
Compare the performance of our framework with state-of-the-art mixed precision optimization methods
Conduct ablation studies to analyze the impact of geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic spectral estimators on the framework's performance
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may not be effective for models with complex or non-convex geometries
The choice of spectral estimators and geometric subspace diagnostics may require careful tuning for optimal performance
The framework's computational overhead may be higher than expected due to the complexity of the geometric subspace diagnostics and spectral estimators
